cityDNA.coreFantasy: A radiant sea-elven coastal academy city where nursery halls, alchemical labs, public works, lecture courts, harbor basilicas, and pearlcurrent amphitheaters make the sea civic and scholarly.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Pelas Mirr is not a generic beautiful coast; the uploaded images show a dense, marble-and-turquoise harbor civilization with nurseries, labs, survey halls, lectures, and ritual harbor infrastructure.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Pelas Mirr turns sea access, nursery care, public works, study, alchemy, and harbor ritual into an organized maritime civic order.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: White limestone, coral stone, sea glass, bronze, polished shell, turquoise tile, blue beacon lamps, colonnades, mosaic plazas, tidal canals, towers, harbor arches, and sea courts.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: Sea-elven culture shaped by scholarship, sea courts, coastal engineering, nursery care, alchemical craft, and public maritime ritual.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Pearl currents, nursery halls, star-map courts, sea glass, blue lamps, permit yards, harbor basilicas, shell arches, and amphitheaters symbolize ordered life beside living water.

canonLocks: Pelas Mirr is a mature sea-elven maritime city, not generic coastal scenery.
The Wonder is Sea-Elven Pearlcurrent Amphitheater.
The Beacon is Nursery Hall Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court.
The five primary districts are Nursery Hall, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, Public Works Authority, University / Lyceum, and Harbor Campus.

The uploaded district anchors include S1 Nursery Hall, Nursery Hall Beacon, S1 Nursery Pillar, S1 Nursery Kiosk, Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, Alchemy Lab, Blue-Lamp Monument, Formula Archive Nook, Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook, University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Lecture Hall, Star Navigation Monument, Observatory Court Nook, S5 Harbor Campus, Harbor Basilica, S5 Harbor Arch, and S5 Harbor Bridgelet.
provisionalCanon: Pelas Mirr's highest civic virtue is cultivated continuity.
The contact-sheet pass emphasizes clear turquoise water, bright stone, civic plazas, and organized harbor movement rather than wild island fantasy.
Nursery and public works should be as important as sea-elven elegance.
rejectedReads: harbor beauty without logistics or public works
cautionFlags: Preserve exact S1 Nursery Hall / Pillar / Kiosk and S5 Harbor Campus / Arch / Bridgelet names.
Keep water movement and civic engineering visible.
Do not reduce Pelas Mirr to Luminthalas-by-the-sea.

environment.environmentalLogic: The sea is road, nursery, ritual field, research subject, and engineering problem. Pelas Mirr uses plazas, canals, survey halls, and harbor campuses to make water legible.

forms: Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court
skyline: Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court as authority
harbor towers, survey halls, lecture courts, and harbor basilica rising from turquoise water

civilization.people: Sea-elven citizens are elegant but practical: caregivers, alchemists, surveyors, lecturers, students, harbor clergy, sailors, and engineers all share the same public waterfront.
civilization.governance: The Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court governs through celestial-maritime law, nursery protections, public works permits, harbor rites, and scholarly testimony.
civilization.religion: Harbor basilica rites, pearlcurrent ceremonies, nursery blessings, star navigation, and sea-court processions make maritime life sacred-adjacent.
bodyLanguage: careful handling of shell and glass

streets.appearance: Bright, elegant, and crowded with turquoise canals, white civic buildings, nursery statues, blue lamps, survey tables, lecture plazas, harbor arches, and boats at street level.
streets.movement: Movement follows canals, bridges, seawalls, mosaic streets, lecture terraces, permit yards, nursery halls, and harbor bridgelets.
streets.lighting: Water-reflected daylight brightens plazas by day; blue lamps and warm harbor lanterns define night.
streets.weatherEffects: Sea spray, glare, clear air, and occasional harbor fog shape visibility and movement.
streets.dayCycle: Nursery care, survey permits, lectures, alchemical work, and harbor traffic dominate daytime.
streets.nightCycle: Blue lamps, quiet canals, harbor rites, observatory work, and late dock traffic dominate night.

housing.wealthy: High terraces and sea-court residences with shell windows, private docks, and views toward the amphitheater or High Court.
housing.middle: Canal apartments, scholar homes, surveyor rooms, and dock-family houses around mosaic courts.
housing.poor: Harbor-edge rooms, service courts, shared water access, and small homes under larger sea-wall structures.

districts.name: Nursery Hall
districts.civicPurpose: Protected early life, public continuity, and Beacon-centered civic response.
districts.visualIdentity: S1 Nursery Hall, Nursery Hall Beacon, S1 Nursery Pillar, S1 Nursery Kiosk, bright plazas, caregivers, and turquoise water.

districts.name: Arcane / Alchemical Labs
districts.civicPurpose: Maritime research, formula control, and dangerous useful knowledge.
districts.visualIdentity: Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, Alchemy Lab, Blue-Lamp Monument, Formula Archive Nook, glassware, blue light, and sea-facing terraces.

districts.name: Public Works Authority
districts.civicPurpose: Harbor engineering, permits, surveys, sea-wall maintenance, and canal control.
districts.visualIdentity: Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook, maps, instruments, and water-facing work courts.

districts.name: University / Lyceum
districts.civicPurpose: Public scholarship, star navigation, civic instruction, and maritime education.
districts.visualIdentity: University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Lecture Hall, Star Navigation Monument, Observatory Court Nook, students, teachers, and open plazas.

districts.name: Harbor Campus
districts.civicPurpose: Harbor ritual, public crossing, maritime blessing, and civic waterfront identity.
districts.visualIdentity: S5 Harbor Campus, Harbor Basilica, S5 Harbor Arch, S5 Harbor Bridgelet, docks, boats, shell detail, and blue water.

wonder.name: Sea-Elven Pearlcurrent Amphitheater
wonder.symbolicRole: Sea, speech, learning, music, and civic gathering made visible in pearl-lit stone.
wonder.visualRole: Large coastal amphitheater integrated with turquoise canals, white terraces, and harbor approaches.
wonder.civicRole: Citywide assembly, performance, lecture, maritime ceremony, and public signal interpretation.
storyHooks: A public lecture in the amphitheater becomes a harbor-wide oath.

beacon.name: Nursery Hall Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Continuity and warning joined in a civic signal.
beacon.visualRole: Blue signal tower within bright nursery hall plazas and water-facing streets.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates nursery protection, public works, harbor routes, lectures, and emergency response.
storyHooks: A Beacon shift forces the city to choose between harbor order and nursery protection.

seatOfPower.name: Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Celestial-maritime law and scholarly authority under formal beauty.
seatOfPower.visualRole: High court of white stone, star-map ceilings, sea-glass windows, ceremonial stairs, and civic terraces.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Governs harbor law, nursery protections, public works permits, scholarship, and maritime obligations.
storyHooks: A star-map ceiling marks the old tide law everyone quotes wrong.

majorLandmarks.name: Alchemy Lab
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Blue-lit laboratory with formula archive, glassware, and water-facing terraces.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Research, formula control, and useful risk management.
storyHooks: A formula archive explains why the tide behaves like law.

majorLandmarks.name: Survey Hall
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Survey hall with blue lamp, permit yard, instruments, maps, and harbor overlooks.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Harbor surveys, sea-wall maintenance, water permits, and public works routing.
storyHooks: A permit yard map reveals the harbor has shifted under the city.

majorLandmarks.name: Lecture Hall
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Knowledge offered publicly beside the sea.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Formal lecture hall with star navigation monument, court paving, students, and open water light.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Education, public lessons, navigation training, and civic interpretation.
storyHooks: A lecture reveals the harbor route no official map admits.

majorLandmarks.name: Harbor Basilica
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Sea-facing basilica with harbor arch, bridgelet, shell detail, boats, and turquoise water.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Harbor rites, public blessings, crossings, and mourning ceremonies.
storyHooks: A harbor blessing proves public works is also faith.

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 must show S1 Nursery Hall, Nursery Hall Beacon, S1 Nursery Pillar, S1 Nursery Kiosk, caregivers, water basins, and white coastal plazas.
S2 must show Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, Alchemy Lab, Blue-Lamp Monument, Formula Archive Nook, glassware, reagents, and sea-facing terraces.
S3 must show Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook, instruments, maps, and harbor engineering activity.
S4 must show University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Lecture Hall, Star Navigation Monument, Observatory Court Nook, students, star charts, and public learning.
S5 must show S5 Harbor Campus, Harbor Basilica, S5 Harbor Arch, S5 Harbor Bridgelet, boats, shell details, and ritual waterfront activity.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Keep Sea-Elven Pearlcurrent Amphitheater, Nursery Hall Beacon, and Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court legible together.
Use white limestone, turquoise canals, sea glass, bronze, pearl light, survey yards, lecture courts, nursery halls, and harbor basilica architecture.
requiredVisualElements: Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court
